hero.name: Justin
hero.subtitle: I like to turn complex problems into simple solutions.

about.title: About me
about.primary: I recently graduated with a bachelor in computer science degree in 2026 with 90%+ average in my computer science courses. I did this while being schizophrenic and believing everybody is reading my thoughts 24/7. It wrecked havoc on my life and makes it really hard to think what I want to think. As a result, learning new things becomes much harder as I have a subconscious fear of the people hacking my brain. It all started in February of 2022, but each year after that, the effects on my psyche and thinking has only gotten worse. I believe I'm a victim of a conspiracy involving hundreds, if not thousands of individuals who have been blessed with advanced tech that can read my thoughts, see through my eyes, hear what I hear, and even feel my emotions remotely. I know some of the people involved in this conspiracy in real life and they never show any guilt whatsoever. They even go as far as joining my games online to try to disturb my peace even more.
about.secondary: Looking forward to learning new skills and getting a job.

projects.title: Peter's Dungeon
projects.description: I built a game using the Unity Game Engine and C#. Features include: racing against AI bots, local storage of highscores, skin customization, upgradeable stats, enemies and hazards, custom track maker. Looking forwards to adding more gameplay features in the future.

projects.title: Nightmare simulator
projects.description: I used C++ and the Qt Framework to develop this simulator which you can play by following the instructions in the Github repo. For my Object-Oriented Design course, I focused on clean, maintainable architecture. I leveraged inheritance and polymorphic behavior to manage diverse game entities through unified interfaces. To handle game states and object creation efficiently, I integrated structural design patterns, utilizing the Factory pattern for dynamic spawning and the Singleton pattern for centralized game management.

projects.title: Arcane Arithmetic
projects.description: A collborative group project. JavaFX and FXML was used to build the frontend and Python was used to build the backend. Some of my important contributions: implemented sound, background music, fullscreen, login screen, loading the instructor dashboard and leaderboard using locally stored json files, special toggle mode that lets you bypass questions, linking frontend with python API, timer event when the timer goes to 0 during a question.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: This is where you can describe about your contact. The more you describe about your contact, the more chances you have!
